scope: Pass B full-surface expansion, researched against the official OpenAPI source of truth (github.com/hellosign/hellosign-openapi's openapi-raw.yaml + translations/en.yaml, fetched 2026-07-04) covering all 75 documented operations across 69 paths. 4 read streams (signature_requests, templates, team_members, account) plus 13 new write actions covering every JSON-body-only signature-request/template/team/account lifecycle mutation the engine's declarative write dialect can express. Excluded: every endpoint whose request body requires multipart/form-data (file/document upload — the engine's write dialect supports only json/form(url-encoded)/none body types, never multipart; sending/creating documents, templates, unclaimed drafts, and faxes all require an actual file payload), the entire Fax/Fax Line product surface (a separate, distinct Dropbox Sign entitlement legacy never touched), Bulk Send Job (read-only status polling for a bulk multipart-created send, itself out of scope since the create step is multipart), OAuth token endpoints (connection-lifecycle plumbing, not a data operation), embedded signing/editing URL generation (session tokens for an iframe, not syncable records or mutations), Report creation (async CSV export job kickoff), single-object detail GETs that duplicate an already-covered list stream's record shape, file-download endpoints (binary payloads), and account_verify (a non-mutating existence lookup with no state change, not a genuine write).

excluded.reason: provisions a brand-new Dropbox Sign account under the caller's API app/OAuth client; account-provisioning admin action, not a record mutation on the connected account's own data
looks up whether a Dropbox Sign account exists for a given email address; a read-like existence check with no state change, not a genuine mutation despite being a POST
creates a new OAuth API application (its own client_id/client_secret credential pair); credential-provisioning admin action, out of scope for a data connector
single API-app detail; API-app management is a developer-portal admin concern, not sync-worthy account data. Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
reconfigures an OAuth API application's callback URL/domains/OAuth settings; credential/security-surface admin action
permanently deletes an OAuth API application and invalidates every token issued under it
API-app management is a developer-portal admin concern, not sync-worthy account data. Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
manually re-fires a previously-failed webhook callback delivery; operational/debugging action against Dropbox Sign's own callback-retry queue, not a record mutation. Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage

status/progress polling for a bulk-send job; the only way to CREATE a bulk send job is multipart (bulk_send_with_template/bulk_create_embedded_with_template), which this dialect cannot express, so there is no way to ever populate a job id to poll
same reasoning as the single bulk_send_job GET; the bulk-send creation step itself is multipart-only and out of scope
mints a short-lived, single-use iframe session token/URL for embedded template editing; a session credential, not a syncable record or a persistent mutation
mints a short-lived, single-use iframe session token/URL for embedded signing; a session credential, not a syncable record

Fax is a separate, distinct Dropbox Sign product entitlement (fax numbers/fax sending) unrelated to e-signature; legacy internal/connectors/dropbox-sign never touched it. Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
Fax product entitlement, out of scope; see /fax/{fax_id} GET
fax document PDF/TIFF binary download, not a syncable record; also Fax product entitlement, out of scope
Fax product entitlement, out of scope; see /fax/{fax_id} GET
sending a fax requires a multipart file upload of the document; Fax product entitlement, also out of scope regardless
Fax Line product entitlement (dedicated fax phone number management), out of scope; see /fax/{fax_id} GET
Fax Line product entitlement, out of scope; see /fax/{fax_id} GET
Fax Line product entitlement, out of scope; see /fax/{fax_id} GET
Fax Line product entitlement, out of scope; see /fax/{fax_id} GET
Fax Line product entitlement, out of scope; see /fax/{fax_id} GET
Fax Line product entitlement, out of scope; see /fax/{fax_id} GET
Fax Line product entitlement, out of scope; see /fax/{fax_id} GET
OAuth2 authorization-code token exchange; connection-lifecycle plumbing (this bundle authenticates via a plain API-key Basic auth spec, not OAuth), not a data operation
OAuth2 token refresh; same reasoning as the token-exchange endpoint
kicks off an asynchronous bulk CSV export job of account activity, not a synchronous read/write record operation; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
the templated multi-recipient bulk-send request body is only expressible as multipart/form-data (a CSV/file of recipient data alongside the template reference)
same multipart-only bulk-recipient-data requirement as bulk_create_embedded_with_template

creating a signature request requires uploading the actual document file(s) as multipart/form-data (or a files_url, still only accepted alongside multipart-shaped signer/field metadata in the SDK-generated request); the engine's write dialect has no multipart body_type
same reasoning as create_embedded: the request accepts multipart/form-data for custom field/signer data alongside file references; not expressible in json/form body_type
re-uploads/replaces the request's document files as multipart/form-data
same file re-upload requirement as edit/{signature_request_id}
same file/custom-field multipart requirement as create_embedded_with_template
same file/custom-field multipart requirement as create_embedded_with_template
signed-document PDF/ZIP binary download, not a syncable record stream
signed-document binary encoded as a data URI string; still a binary payload, not a syncable record stream
mints a short-lived download URL for the signed-document binary; not a syncable record stream
single-signature-request detail; identical record shape to the signature_requests list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream

creating a signature request requires uploading the actual document file(s) as multipart/form-data (or a files_url alongside multipart-shaped signer metadata); the engine's write dialect has no multipart body_type

the request body for custom field values / CC recipients is only fully expressible as multipart/form-data in Dropbox Sign's own SDK-generated shape (array-of-object custom_fields alongside optional multipart file additions)

permanently deletes the caller's entire Team (only invocable when the Team has exactly one member); irreversible account-structure change with no per-record scope, unlike the other team actions which target a specific member/name
team_members stream already reads GET /team/members/{team_id}, which returns the same roster this endpoint's response embeds; not modeled as a separate stream to avoid a duplicate record shape
team subscription/plan metadata bundled with the same team roster team_members already reads; not a distinct record shape worth a separate stream
pending-invite list for the caller's Team; a narrow admin-facing sub-list, not modeled as a separate stream this pass. Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
Enterprise-plan sub-team hierarchy listing, a distinct organizational feature legacy never modeled. Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
creating a template requires uploading the actual document file(s) as multipart/form-data; the engine's write dialect has no multipart body_type
same file-upload requirement as template/create, embedded-editor variant
template document PDF binary download, not a syncable record stream
template document binary encoded as a data URI string; still a binary payload
mints a short-lived download URL for the template document binary; not a syncable record stream
single-template detail; identical record shape to the templates list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
content update variant that accepts multipart/form-data for updated signer/field metadata alongside the existing files; not expressible in json/form body_type
replaces the template's document file(s) via multipart/form-data upload
creating an unclaimed draft requires uploading the actual document file(s) as multipart/form-data; the engine's write dialect has no multipart body_type
same file-upload requirement as unclaimed_draft/create, embedded variant
multipart-shaped custom field/signer data requirement, same reasoning as signature_request/create_embedded_with_template

re-edits and re-sends a request originally created from an unclaimed draft; couples to the unclaimed-draft creation flow this connector does not implement (multipart-only), so there is no way for a synced record to ever supply a valid target here
